title: Codereview pass 1: MockRng SAFETY comment justifies data races rather than ruling them out
description:
Location: rust/lichen-embassy/src/mock.rs:146-151

The SAFETY comment claims data races are 'acceptable' rather than establishing they cannot occur. Rust's UB rules don't have an 'acceptable data race' exception - a data race on static mut is UB regardless of consequence severity.

Better justification would be:
- Assert single-threaded usage is enforced (e.g., #[cfg(not(test))] compile_error on multi-threaded test frameworks)
- Or use AtomicU64 with Relaxed ordering (zero overhead on most platforms)
- Or acknowledge UB risk explicitly: 'SAFETY: We accept potential UB from data races because...'

Current comment reads as if 'acceptable outcome' justifies unsafe, which sets a bad pattern.

Priority: P3 - The risk is real but low (test-only code, single-threaded in practice). The comment exists and mostly explains the reasoning.
close_reason: SAFETY comment observation - documented behavior is acceptable for test-only code
